name: materialize_cut
description: Encode a trim spec or raw video segment into an H.264 clip. Uses input-level seeking (-ss/-t before -i) so only the requested segment is decoded — no full file decode regardless of position. Used in workflows that require an actual video file before a subsequent step (e.g. remove_bg). CONCURRENCY WARNING: each instance is a full libx264 encode — memory-intensive at 4K. Use --inputs (batch mode) which caps concurrency at 2 workers by default. Never fan out more than 2–3 instances in parallel; unlike analysis steps (waveform_trim, rm_nonspeech) this step has a hard concurrency ceiling.

input.description: Trim spec JSON (from waveform_trim / rm_fillers / etc.) or raw video file

output.description: Encoded H.264 clip with all cuts applied. Batch mode (--inputs) returns a JSON array of output paths.

params.name: input
params.description: Single trim spec JSON or video file (mutually exclusive with inputs)

params.name: inputs
params.description: Multiple trim specs or video files. Preferred over running separate instances in parallel — concurrency is capped internally at --workers (default 2) to prevent memory exhaustion on 4K footage.

params.name: inpoint
params.description: Keep from this source time (seconds). Used with raw video --input only.

params.name: outpoint
params.description: Keep to this source time (seconds). Used with raw video --input only.

params.name: cuts
params.description: JSON [[start,end],...] — time ranges to remove. Used with raw video --input only. Can be combined with --inpoint/--outpoint.

params.name: workers
params.description: Max concurrent encodes in batch mode. Default 2. Do not raise above 3 for 4K footage — each encode holds multiple decoded frame buffers in memory.

params.name: out
params.description: Output path (default: {original_stem}_cut.mp4 next to source). --input only.
